chunks.content:
Article: Release date of Evangelion 111 Blu Ray in Italy
[]: Release date of Evangelion 1.11 Blu-Ray in Italy             Quote Post by Tetsuo87 » Thu Aug 27, 2009 7:32 am yesterday the Italian publisher Dynit announced the date of release of Evangelion 1.11 In Blu-Ray. public statement. (Automatically translated): "Blu-ray Evangelion 1:11 - the first Blu-ray Dynit could only be Evangelion! The latest edition of Evangelion 1.0 from digital master and previously unreleased footage. Outbox in late October at a recommended price of € 30. First edition with deluxe packaging limited edition. See you soon for updates!"

[]: Re: release date of Evangelion 1.11 Blu-Ray in Italy             Quote Post by ath » Thu Aug 27, 2009 8:45 am Tetsuo87 wrote: Besides this, the same day there will be a release (from official website Dynit): "Neon Genesis Evangelion The Feature Film - The new edition [is Revival of Evangelion NdR.] of" Death & Rebirth and The End of Evangelion, with video and audio restored DTS 5.1! limited edition with 50 page booklet about. "

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Thu Aug 27, 2009 9:26 am ^ @ath: Heheh, I was about to drop you a link to this post with the happy news in my reply to that other Renewal-related thread... (I'm really curious about the 1.01 dub BTW). Thank you for the news, Tetsuo87 !

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Thu Aug 27, 2009 9:58 am LiLi wrote: ^ @ath: Heheh, I was about to drop you a link to this post with the happy news in my reply to that other Renewal-related thread... (I'm really curious about the 1.01 dub BTW).

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Thu Aug 27, 2009 10:33 am ath wrote: It's not bad, as much of the cast is the same as the original series, but they have grown older and have slightly different voices (this is especially noticeable with Rei, which sounds nothing like a 14-old anymore).

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Thu Aug 27

Article: Release date of Evangelion 111 Blu Ray in Italy
 the same as the original series, but they have grown older and have slightly different voices (this is especially noticeable with Rei, which sounds nothing like a 14-old anymore).

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Thu Aug 27, 2009 10:57 am LiLi wrote: (I don't remember the exact words, but IIRC she said working on NGE hadn't been much fun, since she likes going all out with acting, but in Rei's case she wasn't, apparently, supposed to act at all - on the contrary, she had to repress herself. (I could kinda see where she's coming from there but, honestly, this made me wonder about the dub director's take on Rei...)

[]:             Quote Post by LiLi » Thu Aug 27, 2009 11:04 am ath wrote: Well, Rei is supposed to speak like an emotionless doll, right? ;) Her performance in the original series was usually good, only a bit of hesitation at a times. I just loved her take on the dialogue between Rei I, II and III in #25, her "three" voices where pretty convincing.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Thu Aug 27, 2009 11:32 am LiLi wrote: Personally, I always had fun imitating Rei's voice (I guess it's different on the job though...)

[]:             Quote Post by Tetsuo87 » Thu Aug 27, 2009 12:19 pm ath wrote: LiLi wrote: Personally, I always had fun imitating Rei's voice (I guess it's different on the job though...)

It looks like it has a completely new dub done by the original voice actors who worked on the series, but I wasn't terribly impressed by their recent 1.0 dub... we'll see how it goes.

 done by the original voice actors who worked on the series, but I wasn't terribly impressed by their recent 1.0 dub... we'll see how it goes.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Wed Oct 21, 2009 5:09 pm LiLi wrote: Wait, does it mean they brought back the first voice actor for Kaworu?

[]:             Quote Post by Tetsuo87 » Sun Nov 01, 2009 3:24 am yesterday was a press conference in Lucca of Dynit, Carlo Cavazzoni also talked about the BD release of Eva. Fabrizio Mazzotta Dubbing director has confirmed the completion of its work on new material (3 minutes!) And blames the delay to the fact that the erroneous material came from Japan. eva for now remains the only title in BD edit 01/04/2010: the date of distribution of BluRay has been postponed to April 21, 2010 caused, as explained by Carlo Cavazzoni at "Lucca Comics", the delay of materials from Japan. specifications of BD it is available here in italian or here in English
